parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.x diff fully deletes scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines removed, 0 added) while simultaneously modifying at least five other modules that may have consumed it. The commit that performed this deletion explicitly references fixing a ReferenceError — proving import cleanup was incomplete at least once before. Without positive evidence that every static and dynamic import of process.mjs has been excised from glm-client.mjs, glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, state.mjs, job-control.mjs, and friends, this change carries a real risk of hard ESM module-resolution crashes at command entry points.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs leaves potential dangling imports across modified consumer modules
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was fully removed as part of the 'ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge' (Bundle F). The commit message itself documents that a ReferenceError existed — meaning imports pointed at this module and were not cleaned up before that fix. In the same cumulative diff, glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed), glm-companion.mjs (139 lines changed), render.mjs (79 lines changed), state.mjs (32 lines changed), and job-control.mjs (20 lines changed) were all modified. If any of these still contain a static import (`import ... from './process.mjs'`), a dynamic import, or a re-export referencing the deleted module, the Node.js ESM loader will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at import-evaluation time — crashing the entire command before any user code runs. Subsequent hotfix commits ('Bundle D3+ post-review hotfixes', '5 bugs from 3-agent full-project review') indicate the purge required multiple remediation rounds, increasing the likelihood that an import reference survived in a less-tested code path. Existing unit tests exercise individual modules but may not cover every import branch, especially dynamic imports gated by runtime conditions.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs' scripts/` to confirm zero remaining references. If clean, add a CI gate that validates all import targets resolve to existing files (e.g., a check-no-broken-imports.sh script). Additionally, exercise every slash-command entry point (`/glm:review`, `/glm:setup`, `/glm:status`, `/glm:rescue`) in a clean install to confirm no ESM resolution failures.

next_steps: Grep the entire scripts/ tree for any remaining reference to process.mjs — static imports, dynamic imports, and string literals
Inspect glm-client.mjs, glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, state.mjs, and job-control.mjs for imports of the deleted module
Run every command entry point in a clean checkout to confirm no module resolution errors
If no dangling imports exist, add a pre-merge verification step to prevent future regressions of this class
